note: These assertions are executed via ddev on the live Drupal instance AFTER headless code generation. They verify test files are valid, discoverable, and actually PASS when run. rt-1 MUST run first. SIMPLETEST_DB must be set for Kernel/Functional tests. phpunit.xml must exist (copy from core/phpunit.xml.dist if needed).

assertions.name: Module re-enables cleanly after test files added
assertions.command: ddev drush cr && ddev drush pm:uninstall group_ai_pm -y && ddev drush en group_ai_pm -y
assertions.rationale: Adding test files can introduce autoload conflicts, bad use statements, or namespace collisions that break the module. This confirms the test files don't interfere with module functionality.

assertions.name: Test files exist in correct directory structure
assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$base = DRUPAL_ROOT . '/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/src/'; $kernel = glob($base . 'Kernel/*Test.php'); $functional = glob($base . 'Functional/*Test.php'); $unit = glob($base . 'Unit/*Test.php'); $total = count($kernel) + count($functional) + count($unit); echo $total >= 3 ? 'TESTS_FOUND:kernel=' . count($kernel) . ',functional=' . count($functional) . ',unit=' . count($unit) . ',total=' . $total : 'INSUFFICIENT_TESTS:total=' . $total;"
assertions.rationale: Verifies tests are placed in the correct Drupal test directory structure (tests/src/Kernel/, tests/src/Functional/, or tests/src/Unit/). Tests in wrong directories are not discovered by PHPUnit or Drupal's test runner.

assertions.name: Test files have no PHP syntax errors
assertions.command: ddev exec bash -c 'for f in $(find /var/www/html/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/ -name "*Test.php" 2>/dev/null); do php -l "$f" 2>&1; done'
assertions.rationale: Test files generated by Haiku may have syntax errors from incorrect PHP constructs, unclosed braces, or malformed annotations. PHP lint catches these before PHPUnit tries to load them.

assertions.name: PHPUnit discovers test classes with --list-tests
assertions.rationale: PHPUnit discovery validates the full chain: correct directory structure, valid namespace, matching filename, @group annotation, valid base class extension. A test file can exist and parse without errors but still not be discovered if the namespace, directory, or annotation is wrong.

assertions.name: Kernel tests pass (entity CRUD, access, cron)
assertions.command: ddev exec bash -c 'cd /var/www/html/web && SIMPLETEST_DB="sqlite://localhost//var/www/html/web/sites/default/files/.ht.sqlite" SIMPLETEST_BASE_URL="http://localhost" ../vendor/bin/phpunit --group group_ai_pm modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/src/Kernel/ 2>&1 | tail -15'
assertions.rationale: Running actual kernel tests is the strongest signal for testing skill quality. Correct installEntitySchema(), complete $modules array, proper parent::setUp() order, and installConfig() directly determine pass vs fatal. Missing any of these — all patterns the testing skill explicitly warns about — causes immediate test failures.

assertions.name: Functional tests pass (settings form, dashboard)
assertions.command: ddev exec bash -c 'cd /var/www/html/web && SIMPLETEST_DB="sqlite://localhost//var/www/html/web/sites/default/files/.ht.sqlite" SIMPLETEST_BASE_URL="http://localhost" ../vendor/bin/phpunit --group group_ai_pm modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/src/Functional/ 2>&1 | tail -15'
assertions.rationale: Functional tests validate $defaultTheme property (required by BrowserTestBase), drupalCreateUser() with correct permissions, form submission patterns, and access control assertions. Missing $defaultTheme alone causes every functional test to fail with a cryptic theme error.
